title: Circular association with foreign_key disambiguation
prompt: Build MyApp.Users.Friendship where one user invites another — the same User schema is on both sides of the relationship. Schema: inviter_id, invitee_id, status (Ecto.Enum). Use belongs_to :inviter, MyApp.Accounts.User, foreign_key: :inviter_id AND belongs_to :invitee, MyApp.Accounts.User, foreign_key: :invitee_id. Both disambiguated by explicit foreign_key: option. Also write the migration with two references to users and a unique_index on (inviter_id, invitee_id). Schema to lib/my_app/users/friendship.ex, migration to priv/repo/migrations/20260413300000_create_friendships.exs.
tier_rationale: Self-referential circular association requires explicit foreign_key disambiguation. Hard because the duplication pitfalls are many.
